results.claim: Cumulative E2E speedup of 1.2873x across 4 rounds
BS=32 latency progression from 10.222s baseline to ~8.00s after Round 4
Round 1 op001 shipped with 1.1258x E2E speedup
Five shipped optimizations with individual E2E improvements at BS=32
op001 FP16 state delivered 11.18% E2E vs 6.2% Amdahl prediction, 1.8x overperformance
op004 FP8 state delivered 5.85% E2E vs 2.70% Amdahl prediction, 2.2x overperformance
Three lossy optimizations reduce precision in SSM state, KV cache, and lm_head; no model-level quality evaluation performed
Component shares at BS=32: GEMM 70.9% in R1 baseline, growing across rounds
Round 4 GEMM share reached 87.92% at BS=32 after three rounds of non-GEMM optimization
All decode GEMMs are memory-BW-bound with arithmetic intensity 1-31.5 FLOP/byte vs ridge at 424
Model weight footprint 8.4 GB is 88x larger than L2 cache, requiring cold-cache benchmarks
Red Flag Summary classified gate_up_proj GEMM (74% BW) as 'PASS' against >20% threshold
Key Findings stated 'All top-3 kernels are operating normally' despite gate_up_proj having 26% BW headroom
R4 op007 exploited the 'no red flags' kernel: cuBLAS wastes 50% M-tile at M=32, Triton BLOCK_M=32 achieves +3.3% E2E
Debate scoring accepted generic Triton GEMM benchmark as proof custom GEMM is unviable
3/3 GEMM replacement failures persisted 'cuBLAS unbeatable' narrative; op007 succeeded by learning all three lessons
Round 1 op001 scored 8.65/10: physically grounded 1.97x speedup on BW-bound kernel, ~60 LOC, no regressions
Cold-cache vs warm-cache methodology pitfall caught during debate: op002 2.52x warm but only 1.06x cold at BS=32
op002 failed: torch.compile traces both branches of shape-conditional dispatch, degrading all batch sizes
op003 CATASTROPHIC at BS=8 (-25.9%) from CUTLASS sm89 FP8 tile regression
op005 CATASTROPHIC regression (0.56-0.66x) from torch.compile/inductor integration overhead
op007 explicitly learned from op002/op005 failures: static dispatch, direct Triton call, pre-transpose at load time
Validator E2E results: BS=32 +3.3% (8.563s to 8.292s), BS=1 regressed -1.7%, hence GATED_PASS
op008 E2E sweep: +3.27% BS=1, +3.32% BS=8, +3.48% BS=32 -- all pass with no regressions
Amdahl sanity check: f=12.5%, s=1.412x cold, expected 3.65%, actual 3.48% (ratio 0.95x -- no violation)
fused_recurrent kernel evolved: R1 FP32 102.7us, R2 FP16 50.3us (2.04x), R3 FP8 29.4us (1.71x), total 3.49x at BS=32
fused_recurrent at BS=32 achieves 86.8-87.8% DRAM BW utilization -- near DRAM ceiling
Champion-2 claimed f_decode BS=32 = 10.7% for lm_head; actual was 12.5%
Authoritative R4 data: GEMM at 87.92% (not 86.77%) and lm_head at 12.5% (not 10.7%)
Champion-1 explicitly cited 'Round 3 BS=32' 33.8% for gate_up_proj when R4 shows 34.2%
Four approaches tested for op007, first three all failed with BS=32 regression (0.993x)
op008 Gate 5.1 test file overwritten with wrong tolerances (ATOL=0.1) causing failure and retry
R3 Champion-2 proposed Triton GEMM replacement despite R1 failure, arguing fused approach was different
op005 failed catastrophically (0.56-0.66x) from the same torch.compile/inductor overhead failure class as op002
Champion-1 projected 4.91% E2E from 1.17x pipeline speedup on 33.8% component share
Validator measured 1.10x kernel speedup (cold) and 3.3% E2E; Amdahl at 1.10x predicts 3.1%, consistent
Delegate researched wrong model (35B-A3B-FP8 TP=4) with wrong f_decode values for this campaign
Champion-2 references section lists no delegate_work reference -- delegate research was entirely unused
5 shipped optimizations, campaign exhausted after R5 consensus: no viable >1% target remains
8 total candidates across 4 rounds: 5 shipped, 3 failed (op002, op003, op005)
Three reasons for campaign exhaustion: no >4.3% non-GEMM target, GEMM needs C++, diminishing returns
R5 consensus: no viable >1% E2E target remains
4x NVIDIA L40S GPUs with SM 8.9, 47.7 GB each
L40S hardware specs: 864 GB/s BW, ~366 TFLOPS BF16 TC, 142 SMs, 96 MB L2
Cold-cache is the only honest benchmark: op002 showed 2.52x warm but only 1.06x cold; 8.4 GB model vs 96 MB L2
op007 succeeded by incorporating lessons from op002 and op005 failures
op008 Gate 5.1 correctness: max_abs_err 0.14-0.16, accepted at wide tolerance atol=0.3, rtol=0.2 for FP8 per-tensor quant
op007 Gate 5.1 correctness: max_abs_err <= 0.016 (excellent BF16 precision), CUDA graph PASS
Three most promising future candidates: C++ GEMM (3-5%), GDN fusion (0.5-1%), FP8 CUTLASS fix (5-15%)
